title: Unit tests for canonicalize_display_name
description: Why: security invariant must be verified by tests with external oracles. What: add unit tests to relay (same module as implementation). Test cases — oracle is the Unicode spec + acceptance criteria: (1) clean_name_passes: 'Alice' -> Ok('Alice'); (2) rtl_override_rejected: 'group\u{202E}name' -> Err; (3) rlo_rejected: 'a\u{202E}b' -> Err; (4) zws_stripped_silently: 'alice\u{200B}bob' -> Ok('alicebob'); (5) nfc_normalizes_homoglyph_form: NFC('e\u{0301}') -> Ok('é') (composed form); (6) name_33_chars_rejected: 'a'.repeat(33) -> Err; (7) name_32_chars_passes: 'a'.repeat(32) -> Ok; (8) empty_rejected; (9) whitespace_only_rejected. Each test must have its expected value from an external oracle (Unicode spec), NOT derived from the function under test.
acceptance_criteria: All 9+ tests pass; no test uses code under test as its own oracle
close_reason: unit tests for canonicalize_display_name pass; external oracles documented
